todayItems.title: Why Shopify’s Earnings Report Sparked a Massive Stock Selloff
todayItems.summary: Shopify  stock took a steep drop on Tuesday after the e-commerce platform reported top-line growth but weak earnings in the first quarter.  Shares fell 17% and are now off 34% for the year after a fourth-quarter earnings miss in February.  Fears about the impact of artificial intelligence on software haven’t helped.

sevenDayItems.title: PayPal Stock Drops Despite Strong Results. Is This a Buying Opportunity?
sevenDayItems.summary: Paypal (NASDAQ: PYPL) came into Q1 2026 as a company in the middle of a reset and still managed to beat across the board. Revenue landed at $8.35 billion versus expectations of $8.05 billion. Adjusted EPS came in at $1.34, comfortably ahead of the $1.27 estimate. Total payment volume hit $464 billion, up 11% year over year, the highest in its history. And yet, the stock fell almost 9% before the market even opened.
